findings.finding: publish_event()'s db parameter gates all DB persistence and dead-letter recording. Every call site in app/ (12) and every call in tests/test_event_bus.py omits db, so it always defaults to None. subscribe() (in-process handler registry) has zero real callers anywhere.
findings.evidence: grep -rn "publish_event(" app --include="*.py" returns 12 call sites, none containing db=; tests/test_event_bus.py calls are exclusively publish_event(evt, db=None). event_bus/bus.py:42 subscribe() is defined but never called in app/ (only activity_stream.py's distinct .subscribe() method is used).
findings.production_impact: events/failed_events DB tables are permanently empty in production; get_unprocessed_events() (replay-on-restart) always returns [] and additionally has zero real callers. The retry-then-dead-letter design is unreachable since _subscribers is always empty. Does not affect user-visible functionality (task/epic status persists via separate direct DB writes in manager.py; real-time UI uses the independent activity_stream.py SSE mechanism), but the entire Postgres-backed event-bus subsystem described in this module's own docstring has no real effect beyond logging and optional Redis Streams fan-out.
findings.recommendation: Either thread a real AsyncSession into every publish_event() call site so persistence/replay/dead-letter actually work, or deliberately remove _persist_event/_write_failed_event/get_unprocessed_events/the events+failed_events tables and update the module docstring to stop claiming DB-backed replay.
findings.fix_note: db: AsyncSession threaded through run_manager() (new param) and passed at all 11 direct publish_event() call sites (9 in manager.py, 2 in api/epics.py); both real callers of run_manager() updated to pass their in-scope db. FleetBus's 12th (indirect) call site left non-persisting by design -- documented limitation, not a gap.

findings.finding: The Fleet OS to legacy-bus forward wraps asyncio.get_event_loop() in a bare try/except RuntimeError: pass, then only forwards if loop.is_running(). asyncio.get_event_loop() raises RuntimeError inside any asyncio.to_thread() worker (no loop ever set there). run_agent_graph() -- which contains every publish(task_started/...) call in base_graph.py -- is invoked via asyncio.to_thread at every real production call site (api/agents.py:667 for simple mode; manager.py's 4 to_thread dispatches of run_backend_dev/run_frontend_dev/run_qa/run_reviewer for full mode).
findings.evidence: fleet_events.py:240-247 (loop=None; try/except RuntimeError: pass; if loop and loop.is_running(): asyncio.create_task(...)); api/agents.py:667 (await asyncio.to_thread(run_coder, ...)); coder.py:144 confirms run_coder calls run_agent_graph() directly (same thread, not further wrapped).
findings.production_impact: For the majority of Fleet OS lifecycle events (TaskStarted/TaskCompleted/TaskFailed/HealthUpdated/LessonPublished), the legacy-bus forward silently never fires -- independent of and compounding ARCH-01-001.
findings.recommendation: Capture the main event loop reference before spawning the to_thread call and use call_soon_threadsafe/run_coroutine_threadsafe to schedule the forward, or move the forward out of publish() into an async wrapper called explicitly from the async call sites after their to_thread call returns.
findings.fix_note: Added set_main_loop()/_main_loop to fleet_events.py, called from main.py lifespan startup on the real main loop; forward now uses run_coroutine_threadsafe against the captured loop (falls back to get_running_loop()+create_task if lifespan never ran). Live-verified with a standalone to_thread-worker reproduction -- event now reaches the legacy bus.

findings.finding: client.messages.create(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", ...) is a hardcoded model string used to verify a user-supplied Anthropic API key, bypassing config.py/model_router.py -- the only such literal found outside those files.
findings.production_impact: Low immediate risk (key-verification ping, not a production inference path), but silently breaks key verification for all users if this exact model string is ever deprecated, and violates CLAUDE.md's permanent Zero Hardcoding rule.
findings.recommendation: Replace the literal with get_settings().model_router (or the Haiku-tier config field), matching the pattern used everywhere else in the codebase.

findings.finding: Pinned but never imported anywhere in app/ or tests/. The codebase calls the anthropic SDK directly, not through LangChain's wrapper.
findings.evidence: grep -rln "langchain_anthropic|langchain\.anthropic|from langchain" app tests --include="*.py" returns zero files.
findings.production_impact: Unnecessary supply-chain surface for zero functional benefit; could mislead a future engineer about the actual LLM-call mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Remove from requirements.txt unless a concrete near-term use is planned; if kept intentionally, add a rationale comment matching this project's own convention.
findings.fix_note: Explicitly deferred per user instruction (2026-07-24) -- not addressed this round.

fix_verification.pytest: 2758 passed, 0 failed, 55 skipped, 17 deselected (unchanged from pre-fix baseline)

not_found: Circular imports at module top-level (2 candidate cycles found via AST scan, both traced to deferred/lazy function-body imports on both sides -- not real import-time cycles)
task_id/trace_id regressions at the 4 previously-documented base_graph.py sites -- fix confirmed still holding

New orphan agent/fleet modules beyond the previously-documented and already-fixed set (versioned_memory.publish, fleet_checkpoint, tool_discovery all confirmed wired; prompt_registry.deploy confirmed still intentionally unused per main.py:181 comment)
CORS/event-bus-retries/Groq-retries hardcoding regressions -- all 3 previously-documented fixes confirmed still config-driven
Two-mode parity regression in launch_coder() (bootstrap check + blocked-transition-on-exception) -- confirmed still present, no regression since Days 11-15 gap closure
Migration chain gaps -- 001 through 019 confirmed linear and gapless by reading actual revision/down_revision values
